+// Pure prompt-classification heuristics extracted so they can be unit-tested
+// without the @github/copilot-sdk/extension runtime.  extension.mjs re-exports
+// everything it needs from here.
+
+export const EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS = [
+  "review",
+  "code review",
+  "review this",
+  "review the code",
+  "review my code",
+  "audit",
+  "approval readiness",
+  "approval readiness checks",
+  "approve this",
+  "pr review",
+  // Bug finding, regression hunting, and approval-readiness are explicit review asks per SKILL.md
+  "find bugs",
+  "bug finding",
+  "regression hunting",
+  "regression hunt",
+  "find regressions",
+];
+
+export const PLAN_KEYWORDS = [
+  "plan",
+  "design",
+  "architecture",
+  "architect",
+  "proposal",
+  "strategy",
+  "approach",
+];
+
+export const DEBUG_KEYWORDS = [
+  "debug",
+  "fix",
+  "failure",
+  "failing",
+  "error",
+  "crash",
+  "root cause",
+  "broken",
+  "investigate",
+];
+
+export const IMPLEMENT_KEYWORDS = [
+  "implement",
+  "build",
+  "create",
+  "write",
+  "refactor",
+  "add",
+  "change",
+  "update",
+  "edit",
+  "enhance",
+  "modify",
+  "adjust",
+  "improve",
+  "fix the code",
+  "fix the formatting",
+  "fix the logic",
+];
+
+export const LIGHT_KEYWORDS = [
+  "explain",
+  "summarize",
+  "what is",
+  "where is",
+  "find",
+  "list",
+  "show",
+  "search",
+];
+
+export const TOOL_KEYWORDS = [
+  "bash",
+  "curl",
+  "execute",
+  "run",
+  "tool",
+  "api",
+  "function",
+  "call",
+  "invoke",
+  "script",
+  "command",
+  "shell",
+  "http",
+  "request",
+  "endpoint",
+  "agent",
+];
+
+// Complex multi-tool orchestration patterns warrant premium-tier escalation per routing-matrix.md.
+// Only multi-word action phrases are included; bare "orchestrate"/"orchestration" caused
+// false positives when tasks merely mentioned these concepts in documentation context.
+export const ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS = [
+  "parallel agents",
+  "multiple agents",
+  "multi-agent",
+  "parallel tools",
+  "tool chain",
+  "chained tools",
+];
+
+export function normalizePrompt(prompt) {
+  return String(prompt || "").toLowerCase();
+}
+
+// Word-boundary-aware matching prevents false positives like "prefix"â†’"fix",
+// "exchange"â†’"change", "callback"â†’"call". Multi-word phrases use substring matching.
+export function matchesPattern(text, pattern) {
+  if (/\s/.test(pattern)) {
+    return text.includes(pattern);
+  }
+  const escaped = pattern.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\{{GIT_DIFF}}");
+  return new RegExp(`\\b${escaped}\\b`).test(text);
+}
+
+export function includesAny(text, patterns) {
+  return patterns.some((pattern) => matchesPattern(text, pattern));
+}
+
+export function isExplicitReviewRequest(text) {
+  return includesAny(text, EXPLICIT_REVIEW_KEYWORDS);
+}
+
+export function isToolHeavy(text) {
+  return includesAny(text, TOOL_KEYWORDS);
+}
+
+export function isComplexOrchestration(text) {
+  return includesAny(text, ORCHESTRATION_KEYWORDS);
+}
+
+export function getComplexity(prompt) {
+  const lower = normalizePrompt(prompt);
+  const words = lower.trim().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length;
+  let score = 0;
+
+  if (words > 40) score += 1;
+  if (words > 100) score += 1;
+  if (
+    includesAny(lower, [
+      "multi-file",
+      "multiple files",
+      "end-to-end",
+      "carefully",
+      "thorough",
+      "deep",
+      "complex",
+      "large",
+      "entire codebase",
+    ])
+  ) {
+    score += 2;
+  }
+
+  if (includesAny(lower, PLAN_KEYWORDS) || includesAny(lower, DEBUG_KEYWORDS)) {
+    score += 1;
+  }
+
+  // File path detection (/.github/, /src/, /lib/, etc.) indicates code changes
+  if (/\/[\w-]+\/[\w.-]+/.test(lower)) {
+    score += 1;
+  }
+
+  // Tool-heavy work deserves more robust models
+  if (isToolHeavy(lower)) {
+    score += 1;
+  }
+
+  return score;
+}
+
+export function classifyPrompt(prompt) {
+  const lower = normalizePrompt(prompt);
+  const complexity = getComplexity(lower);
+
+  if (isExplicitReviewRequest(lower)) {
+    return { kind: "review", complexity, tier: "reasoning" };
+  }
+
+  if (includesAny(lower, PLAN_KEYWORDS)) {
+    return {
+      kind: "planning",
+      complexity,
+      tier: complexity >= 3 ? "reasoning" : "builder",
+    };
+  }
+
+  if (includesAny(lower, DEBUG_KEYWORDS)) {
+    return {
+      kind: "debugging",
+      complexity,
+      tier: complexity >= 3 ? "reasoning" : "builder",
+    };
+  }
+
+  if (includesAny(lower, IMPLEMENT_KEYWORDS)) {
+    // Implementation tasks deserve at least builder tier for reliability
+    // Only use economy for very simple, lightweight implementations
+    let tier = "builder"; // Default to builder for all implementation
+
+    // But if it's a light keyword (find, list, show) with very low complexity, stay in economy
+    if (includesAny(lower, LIGHT_KEYWORDS) && complexity === 0) {
+      tier = "economy";
+    }
+
+    // Escalate to reasoning for complex orchestration only when genuine complexity supports it
+    if (isComplexOrchestration(lower) && complexity >= 2) {
+      tier = "reasoning";
+    }
+
+    // Escalate to reasoning if implementation is deeply complex
+    if (complexity >= 4) {
+      tier = "reasoning";
+    }
+
+    return {
+      kind: "implementation",
+      complexity,
+      tier,
+      toolHeavy: isToolHeavy(lower),
+    };
+  }
+
+  if (includesAny(lower, LIGHT_KEYWORDS) || complexity === 0) {
+    return { kind: "lightweight", complexity, tier: "economy" };
+  }
+
+  let generalTier = complexity >= 2 ? "builder" : "economy";
+  if (isToolHeavy(lower) && complexity >= 1) generalTier = "builder";
+  if (isComplexOrchestration(lower) && complexity >= 2) generalTier = "reasoning";
+  return { kind: "general", complexity, tier: generalTier };
+}

+// Run with: node --test .github/extensions/model-router/classify.test.mjs
+// No external dependencies â€“ uses Node built-ins only (node:test, node:assert).

+import { test, describe } from "node:test";
+import assert from "node:assert/strict";
+
+import {
+  matchesPattern,
+  isExplicitReviewRequest,
+  isToolHeavy,
+  isComplexOrchestration,
+  getComplexity,
+  classifyPrompt,
+  normalizePrompt,
+} from "./classify.mjs";
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// matchesPattern â€“ word-boundary correctness
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+describe("matchesPattern", () => {
+  test("matches a plain word at a word boundary", () => {
+    assert.ok(matchesPattern("please fix the bug", "fix"));
+  });
+
+  test("does not match a bare word inside a longer token", () => {
+    // "prefix" must not trigger the "fix" keyword
+    assert.ok(!matchesPattern("prefix the file", "fix"));
+    // "exchange" must not trigger "change"
+    assert.ok(!matchesPattern("exchange rate logic", "change"));
+    // "callback" must not trigger "call"
+    assert.ok(!matchesPattern("add a callback handler", "call"));
+  });
+
+  test("multi-word phrases use substring matching (no boundary check needed)", () => {
+    assert.ok(matchesPattern("please find bugs in my code", "find bugs"));
+    assert.ok(!matchesPattern("can you find the debug output", "find bugs"));
+  });
+
+  test("is case-sensitive (caller is expected to normalise first)", () => {
+    assert.ok(!matchesPattern("Fix the code", "fix"));
+    assert.ok(matchesPattern("fix the code", "fix"));
+  });
+});
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// isExplicitReviewRequest
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+describe("isExplicitReviewRequest", () => {
+  const positives = [
+    "please review my code",
+    "can you do a code review",
+    "audit this module",
+    "check approval readiness",
+    "pr review for this branch",
+    "find bugs in the payment service",
+    "bug finding pass on the new feature",
+    "regression hunting after the deploy",
+    "find regressions introduced by this PR",
+    "review the code before merging",
+    "review this change",
+  ];
+
+  for (const prompt of positives) {
+    test(`detects review intent: "${prompt}"`, () => {
+      assert.ok(
+        isExplicitReviewRequest(normalizePrompt(prompt)),
+        `Expected "${prompt}" to be detected as a review request`,
+      );
+    });
+  }
+
+  const negatives = [
+    "implement a new feature",
+    "fix the formatting of this file",
+    "explain how the router works",
+    "update the README",
+    "debug the login flow",
+    // "fix" alone is a debug keyword, not a review keyword
+    "fix the failing test",
+  ];
+
+  for (const prompt of negatives) {
+    test(`does not trigger review: "${prompt}"`, () => {
+      assert.ok(
+        !isExplicitReviewRequest(normalizePrompt(prompt)),
+        `Expected "${prompt}" NOT to be detected as a review request`,
+      );
+    });
+  }
+});
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// isToolHeavy
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+describe("isToolHeavy", () => {
+  const positives = [
+    "run the test suite via bash",
+    "call the GitHub API endpoint",
+    "execute the migration script",
+    "invoke the shell command",
+    "make an http request to the service",
+    "use the agent to fetch data",
+  ];
+
+  for (const prompt of positives) {
+    test(`detects tool-heavy: "${prompt}"`, () => {
+      assert.ok(
+        isToolHeavy(normalizePrompt(prompt)),
+        `Expected "${prompt}" to be tool-heavy`,
+      );
+    });
+  }
+
+  const negatives = [
+    "explain the architecture",
+    "summarize the recent changes",
+    "list all open issues",
+  ];
+
+  for (const prompt of negatives) {
+    test(`not tool-heavy: "${prompt}"`, () => {
+      assert.ok(
+        !isToolHeavy(normalizePrompt(prompt)),
+        `Expected "${prompt}" NOT to be tool-heavy`,
+      );
+    });
+  }
+});
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// isComplexOrchestration
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+describe("isComplexOrchestration", () => {
+  const positives = [
+    "run parallel agents to gather results",
+    "use multiple agents to handle the pipeline",
+    "set up a multi-agent workflow",
+    "chain the tools together in a tool chain",
+    "execute chained tools for this task",
+    "run parallel tools across the repos",

+
+  for (const prompt of positives) {
+    test(`detects orchestration: "${prompt}"`, () => {
+      assert.ok(
+        isComplexOrchestration(normalizePrompt(prompt)),
+        `Expected "${prompt}" to be complex orchestration`,
+      );
+    });
+  }
+
+  const negatives = [
+    // Bare "orchestrate" / "orchestration" should NOT trigger (per routing-matrix comment)
+    "orchestrate the deployment",
+    "describe the orchestration pattern",
+    "implement a feature using a tool",
+    "call the function once",
+  ];
+
+  for (const prompt of negatives) {
+    test(`not orchestration: "${prompt}"`, () => {
+      assert.ok(
+        !isComplexOrchestration(normalizePrompt(prompt)),
+        `Expected "${prompt}" NOT to be complex orchestration`,
+      );
+    });
+  }
+});
+

+  test("audit keyword routes to reasoning", () => {

+  });
+
+  test("find bugs routes to reasoning, not implementation", () => {
+    const r = classifyPrompt("find bugs in the payment service");
+    assert.equal(r.kind, "review");

+  test("regression hunting routes to reasoning", () => {

+    assert.equal(r.kind, "review");

+  });
+});

+describe("classifyPrompt â€“ tool-heavy implementation â†’ builder tier, toolHeavy flag", () => {
+  test("implementation with bash keyword â†’ builder, toolHeavy true", () => {
+    const r = classifyPrompt("implement a script that runs bash commands");
+    assert.equal(r.kind, "implementation");
+    assert.equal(r.tier, "builder");
+    assert.equal(r.toolHeavy, true);
+  });
+
+  test("implementation with API keyword â†’ builder, toolHeavy true", () => {
+    const r = classifyPrompt("build a function that calls the GitHub API");
+    assert.equal(r.kind, "implementation");
+    assert.equal(r.tier, "builder");
+    assert.equal(r.toolHeavy, true);
+  });
+
+  test("plain implementation without tool keywords â†’ builder, toolHeavy false", () => {
+    const r = classifyPrompt("implement a new sorting algorithm");
+    assert.equal(r.kind, "implementation");
+    assert.equal(r.tier, "builder");
+    assert.equal(r.toolHeavy, false);
+  });
+
+  test("implementation with light keyword at zero complexity â†’ economy", () => {
+    // "list" is a LIGHT_KEYWORD; with no extra complexity the tier should stay economy
+    const r = classifyPrompt("list and add items");
+    assert.equal(r.kind, "implementation");
+    assert.equal(r.tier, "economy");
+  });
+});
+
+describe("classifyPrompt â€“ orchestration escalation â†’ reasoning tier", () => {
+  test("implementation + orchestration keywords at sufficient complexity â†’ reasoning", () => {
+    // "multiple files" adds 2 complexity, putting us at >= 2 for orchestration check
+    const r = classifyPrompt(

+    assert.equal(r.tier, "reasoning");

+    assert.equal(r.tier, "reasoning");
+  });

+  test("orchestration keyword without enough complexity stays at builder for implementation", () => {
+    // Short prompt with orchestration keyword but complexity < 2 should NOT escalate
+    const r = classifyPrompt("add a multi-agent step");
+    assert.equal(r.kind, "implementation");
+    // complexity: tool-heavy from "agent" (TOOL_KEYWORDS) = 1, not >= 2, so tier stays builder
+    assert.equal(r.tier, "builder");
+  });
+});
+
+describe("classifyPrompt â€“ lightweight and general routing", () => {
+  test("explain question â†’ lightweight, economy", () => {
+    const r = classifyPrompt("explain how the router works");
+    assert.equal(r.kind, "lightweight");
+    assert.equal(r.tier, "economy");
+  });
+
+  test("empty prompt â†’ lightweight, economy", () => {
+    const r = classifyPrompt("");
+    assert.equal(r.tier, "economy");
+  });
+
+  test("planning keyword routes to planning kind", () => {
+    const r = classifyPrompt("design a new caching strategy");
+    assert.equal(r.kind, "planning");
+  });
+
+  test("debug keyword routes to debugging kind", () => {
+    const r = classifyPrompt("debug the failing login flow");
+    assert.equal(r.kind, "debugging");
+  });
+});
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// getComplexity â€“ scoring sanity checks
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+describe("getComplexity", () => {
+  test("short simple prompt scores 0", () => {
+    assert.equal(getComplexity("explain this"), 0);
+  });
+
+  test("tool keyword bumps complexity", () => {
+    const base = getComplexity("implement a feature");
+    const withTool = getComplexity("implement a feature using bash");
+    assert.ok(withTool > base);
+  });
+
+  test('"multiple files" phrase adds 2 to complexity', () => {
+    const base = getComplexity("refactor this module");
+    const withFiles = getComplexity("refactor this module across multiple files");
+    assert.ok(withFiles >= base + 2);
+  });
+
+  test("file path in prompt adds 1 to complexity", () => {
+    const base = getComplexity("update the config");
+    const withPath = getComplexity("update the config in /src/config.js");
+    assert.ok(withPath > base);
+  });
+});
